branch_actions.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
branch_actions.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: No reusable prior manifest exists for this request identity.
branch_actions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

branch_actions.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
branch_actions.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: Exact retry against a terminal manifest returns the persisted decision bundle.
branch_actions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: Exact retry against a still-pre-start sealed manifest reuses the same context.
branch_actions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: A started attempt is recovered under the same attempt lineage.
branch_actions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

branch_actions.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
branch_actions.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: Legally distinct post-terminal continuation is required in the same lineage family.

branch_actions.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
branch_actions.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
branch_actions.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
branch_actions.state_or_outcome: Material request identity change forces a fresh child lineage.
branch_actions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

branch_reason_codes.authority_safety_posture: Nightly continuation branches must explicitly carry NIGHTLY_WINDOW_ADVANCED.
branch_reason_codes.operator_visible_effect: Manifest explorers can explain the legal branch basis with typed reasons instead of free-form copy.
branch_reason_codes.recovery_posture: Typed reasons preserve replay, recovery, terminal retry, and nightly continuity distinctions.
branch_reason_codes.trigger_or_entry_condition: One manifest branch action is chosen and needs a machine-checked legal basis.

claim_atomicity_rules.authority_safety_posture: Queue absence cannot imply execution if durable claim and publication proof are missing.
claim_atomicity_rules.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust one atomic start boundary instead of reconstructing worker-side order.
claim_atomicity_rules.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
claim_atomicity_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

claim_atomicity_rules.authority_safety_posture: Queue absence cannot imply execution if durable claim and publication proof are missing.
claim_atomicity_rules.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust one atomic start boundary instead of reconstructing worker-side order.
claim_atomicity_rules.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
claim_atomicity_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

claim_atomicity_rules.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
claim_atomicity_rules.state_or_outcome: The active claim token, holder, epoch, and expiry are committed together.
claim_atomicity_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

claim_atomicity_rules.authority_safety_posture: Queue absence cannot imply execution if durable claim and publication proof are missing.
claim_atomicity_rules.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust one atomic start boundary instead of reconstructing worker-side order.
claim_atomicity_rules.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
claim_atomicity_rules.state_or_outcome: The first durable stage/outbox publication refs and first_publication_committed_at_or_null are committed together.
claim_atomicity_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

claim_invariants.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.
claim_invariants.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust claim-state semantics across sealed, in-progress, failed, and terminal posture.
claim_invariants.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
claim_invariants.state_or_outcome: `SEALED` manifests are strictly pre-start and therefore `manifest_start_claim.claim_state = UNCLAIMED_SEALED`.
claim_invariants.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

claim_outcomes.authority_safety_posture: No second live start is authorized by broker silence or missing heartbeat alone.
claim_outcomes.operator_visible_effect: Claim results remain typed and visible for scheduler, API, and operator tooling.
claim_outcomes.recovery_posture: All non-granted outcomes are fail-closed for new post-seal execution.
claim_outcomes.state_or_outcome: Start lease granted and run_started committed atomically.
claim_outcomes.trigger_or_entry_condition: A caller attempts to start or reclaim one sealed manifest.

frozen_identity_inputs.authority_safety_posture: Requested and effective scope distinctions remain explicit inside branch proof.
frozen_identity_inputs.operator_visible_effect: Operators can audit why one request was the same request, a replay, a recovery, or a materially new request.
frozen_identity_inputs.recovery_posture: All branch choices remain reproducible from the same frozen identity spine.
frozen_identity_inputs.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection freezes the comparison vector for reuse, replay, recovery, and continuation decisions.

recovery_and_reclaim_rules.authority_safety_posture: Heartbeat loss alone does not authorize second starts or authority replay.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers can prove why reclaim succeeded or failed.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.recovery_posture: Reclaim is explicit and proof-driven rather than heartbeat-driven.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.state_or_outcome: A claim becomes reclaimable only when durable expiry proof exists under frozen policy.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: A stale-lease recovery or reclaim is attempted.

recovery_and_reclaim_rules.authority_safety_posture: Successor lineage cannot silently downgrade stale reclaim into ordinary continuation.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.operator_visible_effect: Lineage explorers show the original attempt and the verified reclaim successor together.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.recovery_posture: Reclaim is same-attempt recovery lineage, not ordinary continuation.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.state_or_outcome: Reclaim preserves the same `attempt_lineage_ref` and marks `claim_state = STALE_RECLAIM_REQUIRED`.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: A stale attempt is reclaimed by a successor.

recovery_and_reclaim_rules.authority_safety_posture: Recovery does not authorize fresh authority mutation if request lineage already left the process.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.operator_visible_effect: Recovery dashboards can point to the exact durable stage that a successor resumed.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.recovery_posture: Resume point is durable publication proof, not queue absence.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.state_or_outcome: Recovery continues from durable stage/outbox publication proof instead of inferring safety from broker silence.
recovery_and_reclaim_rules.trigger_or_entry_condition: A recovery successor resumes an interrupted started manifest.

rows.operator_visible_effect: Claim results remain typed and visible for scheduler, API, and operator tooling.
rows.recovery_posture: All non-granted outcomes are fail-closed for new post-seal execution.
rows.state_or_outcome: Start lease granted and run_started committed atomically.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A caller attempts to start or reclaim one sealed manifest.

rows.state_or_outcome: Manifest is not in a legal pre-start state for new post-seal execution.

rows.authority_safety_posture: No second live start is authorized by broker silence or missing heartbeat alone.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Claim results remain typed and visible for scheduler, API, and operator tooling.
rows.recovery_posture: All non-granted outcomes are fail-closed for new post-seal execution.
rows.state_or_outcome: The persisted attempt cannot restart fresh and must transition through recovery lineage.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A caller attempts to start or reclaim one sealed manifest.

rows.authority_safety_posture: No second live start is authorized by broker silence or missing heartbeat alone.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Claim results remain typed and visible for scheduler, API, and operator tooling.
rows.recovery_posture: All non-granted outcomes are fail-closed for new post-seal execution.
rows.state_or_outcome: Stale reclaim proof succeeded and a verified successor resumed the same attempt lineage.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A caller attempts to start or reclaim one sealed manifest.

rows.authority_safety_posture: No second live start is authorized by broker silence or missing heartbeat alone.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Claim results remain typed and visible for scheduler, API, and operator tooling.
rows.recovery_posture: All non-granted outcomes are fail-closed for new post-seal execution.
rows.state_or_outcome: A reclaim attempt was rejected because the active lease still exists.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A caller attempts to start or reclaim one sealed manifest.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Queue absence cannot imply execution if durable claim and publication proof are missing.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust one atomic start boundary instead of reconstructing worker-side order.
rows.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

rows.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
rows.state_or_outcome: The active claim token, holder, epoch, and expiry are committed together.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Queue absence cannot imply execution if durable claim and publication proof are missing.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust one atomic start boundary instead of reconstructing worker-side order.
rows.recovery_posture: There is no legal state where a run is open but the start claim remains pre-start.
rows.state_or_outcome: The first durable stage/outbox publication refs and first_publication_committed_at_or_null are committed together.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: CLAIM_GRANTED commits under one durable write boundary.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Heartbeat loss alone does not authorize second starts or authority replay.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers can prove why reclaim succeeded or failed.
rows.recovery_posture: Reclaim is explicit and proof-driven rather than heartbeat-driven.
rows.state_or_outcome: A claim becomes reclaimable only when durable expiry proof exists under frozen policy.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A stale-lease recovery or reclaim is attempted.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Successor lineage cannot silently downgrade stale reclaim into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Lineage explorers show the original attempt and the verified reclaim successor together.
rows.recovery_posture: Reclaim is same-attempt recovery lineage, not ordinary continuation.
rows.state_or_outcome: Reclaim preserves the same `attempt_lineage_ref` and marks `claim_state = STALE_RECLAIM_REQUIRED`.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A stale attempt is reclaimed by a successor.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Recovery does not authorize fresh authority mutation if request lineage already left the process.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Recovery dashboards can point to the exact durable stage that a successor resumed.
rows.recovery_posture: Resume point is durable publication proof, not queue absence.
rows.state_or_outcome: Recovery continues from durable stage/outbox publication proof instead of inferring safety from broker silence.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A recovery successor resumes an interrupted started manifest.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust claim-state semantics across sealed, in-progress, failed, and terminal posture.
rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.state_or_outcome: `SEALED` manifests are strictly pre-start and therefore `manifest_start_claim.claim_state = UNCLAIMED_SEALED`.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.

rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.state_or_outcome: Terminal post-start manifests must have `manifest_start_claim.claim_state = TERMINAL_RESULT_RECORDED`.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.

rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.state_or_outcome: `FAILED` started manifests must distinguish reclaim-required posture from terminal failure posture.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust claim-state semantics across sealed, in-progress, failed, and terminal posture.
rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.state_or_outcome: Same-attempt recovery children reuse the original `attempt_lineage_ref`; continuation, replay, and new-request children may not impersonate it.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Invariant violations remain auditable instead of hidden in worker logs.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trust claim-state semantics across sealed, in-progress, failed, and terminal posture.
rows.recovery_posture: Invariant failures block silent duplicate start or ambiguous failure collapse.
rows.state_or_outcome: Audit events and metrics must make claim conflicts, stale-lease recovery, and duplicate-start suppression observable.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest lifecycle and claim state must stay semantically aligned.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Claim state prevents ambiguous live restarts.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers can classify whether a manifest is pre-start, active, reclaimable, or terminal.
rows.recovery_posture: Claim state is durable truth for duplicate-start suppression and reclaim semantics.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest start-claim contract serializes its authoritative lifecycle state.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Status codes keep already-active, reclaim-required, and already-terminal postures distinct.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Claim UI can summarize coarse claim posture without hiding the underlying claim state.
rows.recovery_posture: Status code is stable read-side contract for scheduler and API flows.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: The claim contract reports its coarse-grained legal claim posture.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
rows.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
rows.state_or_outcome: No reusable prior manifest exists for this request identity.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
rows.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
rows.state_or_outcome: Exact retry against a terminal manifest returns the persisted decision bundle.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
rows.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
rows.state_or_outcome: Exact retry against a still-pre-start sealed manifest reuses the same context.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.state_or_outcome: Caller explicitly requested replay and the child preserves replay lineage.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.

rows.state_or_outcome: Legally distinct post-terminal continuation is required in the same lineage family.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Branch decisions may not widen effective scope beyond requested scope or collapse recovery into ordinary continuation.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and APIs can execute one explicit branch action instead of heuristically diffing nearby manifests.
rows.recovery_posture: Branch action keeps terminal retry, pre-start reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, and new request distinct.
rows.state_or_outcome: Material request identity change forces a fresh child lineage.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection resolves one request-time manifest choice.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Nightly continuation branches must explicitly carry NIGHTLY_WINDOW_ADVANCED.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Manifest explorers can explain the legal branch basis with typed reasons instead of free-form copy.
rows.recovery_posture: Typed reasons preserve replay, recovery, terminal retry, and nightly continuity distinctions.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One manifest branch action is chosen and needs a machine-checked legal basis.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Requested and effective scope distinctions remain explicit inside branch proof.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can audit why one request was the same request, a replay, a recovery, or a materially new request.
rows.recovery_posture: All branch choices remain reproducible from the same frozen identity spine.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection freezes the comparison vector for reuse, replay, recovery, and continuation decisions.
